reasoning: My brothers will argue about citizenship and big promises, but I have learned what reaches my hand. The rice card came every month from this government, the medicine for my mother is still covered, my niece received her Rupashree — these are real. The citizenship paper they promised in 2019, I am still waiting for it. Five years, and only fourteen papers have come by now — how many lakhs of us are there? I voted AITC before because what they promised actually arrived in my hand. Shantanu Thakur's citizenship will come when I hold the paper, not before. I vote for what I know is real, not for last-moment promises.
primary_drivers: Khadya Sathi rice and Swasthya Sathi healthcare are tangible and reliable from AITC government
Citizenship papers promised since 2019 still not in hand — only 14 distributed by polling day
Previous voting success with AITC schemes that actually reached her family
